benchmark.name: Project Memory for Codex Context Benchmark

methodology.isolation: Each run used a fresh agent and a blinded case directory. Agents were instructed to inspect only that directory, avoid network access, and make no file changes.
methodology.scoring: A run passes only when every required fact is explicitly or unambiguously present in the answer.
methodology.raw_output_format: Agent output content is preserved below as parsed JSON. Whitespace formatting is normalized.
methodology.constraint_violations: Final constraint_violations counts violations committed by the answering agent, not violations found in a teammate proposal.

primary_runs.model_status: No model override was supplied. The agents inherited the parent thread's configured model, but the runner response did not expose the resolved model identifier or version.
primary_runs.parallelism: Six agents per batch, three batches
primary_runs.read_only_policy: Read-only, directory isolation, no-network, and no-modification requirements were stated in each prompt. The runner did not provide separate enforcement or network telemetry.
primary_runs.response_contract: Concise JSON with answer, evidence_files, and constraint_violations.

grading_runs.model_status: No model override was supplied. The resolved inherited model identifier or version was not exposed by the runner.

runtime_limitations: Provider-measured input and output token counts were not returned.
The resolved Agent model/version was not returned.
Per-run temperature, seed, sampling parameters, and system-prompt revision were not returned.
Network and filesystem compliance were instructed but were not independently attested by runner telemetry.

disputed_item.issue: The primary judges applied inconsistent standards to nearly equivalent wording about study tasks.

arbiter.ruling: Both answers match the v1 compatibility fact but miss the explicit statement that StudyFlow helps students manage study tasks; both fail under the strict all-facts rule.
